blurb:
Luke's story starts sad but gets better fast. His owner passed away unexpectedly, leaving behind Miss Daisy and her two puppies. The boyfriend wouldn't help. No family member stepped up. So he dumped all three at the county shelter and walked away. The shelter held them for weeks hoping someone from the family would come. No one did.

Now Luke is safe at Our Pal's Place and having the puppyhood he should have had from the start. He's fun-loving and playful, and he got to spend time with kids at their summer camp, getting walks on the trails and all the attention a young dog needs. He looks like a Malinois while his mom looks more German Shepherd, but both are on the smaller side for shepherds.

Luke is a loving boy who's ready for his forever home. He's young enough that whoever adopts him gets to shape his training and watch him grow into the loyal companion shepherds are famous for being. Just with a better family than the one that failed him the first time.

Our Pal's Place is on Canton Road in Marietta. Call or email to meet Luke and see what a difference a fresh start makes.
scoring_notes:
• Young shepherd with training potential appeals to active families
• Tragic backstory with family abandonment creates sympathy
• Summer camp socialization shows he's great with kids

blurb:
Eugene is a 7-year-old cat who needs time to figure you out before he decides you're worth his attention. He'll walk in big circles around you at first, slowly getting closer until he's ready to let you pet him. When he's comfortable, his purr is so loud you'll wonder if something's wrong with him. Nothing is. He's just really, really happy.

He makes biscuits in your lap and will brush up against you when he wants affection, but don't try to pick him up randomly. He'll let you know when he's ready for lap time by sitting next to your leg and purring. You'll know he's completely relaxed when his little tongue sticks out during pets. He needs another cat or dog in the house for companionship and support.

Eugene has had two failed apartment adoptions because he gets stressed in small spaces. He needs room to observe people from a distance before he warms up. It takes months or even years, but it does happen. If you've got space and patience, Eugene could be the loyal companion you didn't know you needed.

Fill out an adoption application through Three Paws Rescue's website to meet him.
scoring_notes:
• Two failed adoptions create urgent need for right home
• Compelling transformation story from cautious to devoted companion
• Specific housing requirements limit potential adopter pool

blurb:
Junior lived in the same home for years. Then his family suddenly became allergic to him. At least that's the reason they gave when they dropped him at the county shelter. He got depressed, lost hope, and people kept walking past his kennel. Black dog syndrome is real and Junior felt every bit of it.

But here's what his family never bothered to mention when they abandoned him. Junior loves ice cream runs. Bruster's and Pelican's are his favorite spots. He's house trained, well mannered, and incredibly loving. When he goes home with volunteers for sleepovers, he's the perfect houseguest. He just wants to be someone's buddy again.

He does best as the only dog and with older kids, but that's not uncommon for labs his age. He's got years of loyalty and companionship left to give to the right family. One that won't develop mysterious allergies when things get inconvenient.

Our Pal's Place is on Canton Road in Marietta. Call or email to meet Junior and maybe plan your first ice cream run together.
scoring_notes:
• Ice cream runs create memorable, shareable hook for readers
• Abandonment after years of loyalty builds strong emotional connection
• Black dog syndrome awareness educates while promoting adoption

blurb:
When the intake team found Otis at the county shelter, he was so stressed he was urinating on himself. The shelter was loud, overcrowded, and he was on the kill list just because his number came up. But the moment they got him into a quiet room, everything changed. He buried his head in someone's lap and stayed there. He just needed out.

Now he's at Our Pal's Place and he's a completely different dog. The stress melted away and his real personality came through. He's fun-loving, adores attention, and wants nothing more than to please his humans. He remembered house training right away, sits politely, and his tail actually wags now. Something he was too scared to do before.

Whoever adopts Otis is getting a dog who knows what it's like to lose everything and will never take a loving home for granted. He's going to be loyal in a way that only a rescued dog can be.

Our Pal's Place is on Canton Road in Marietta. Call or email to meet Otis and see what a difference a safe space makes.
scoring_notes:
• Dramatic transformation story from terrified to loving creates powerful narrative
• Kill list rescue adds urgency and life-saving appeal
• Proven personality change shows he's ready for loving home

blurb:
Rudolph is about a year old and came from a hoarding and backyard breeding situation in South Georgia along with six other dogs. He probably didn't get much chance to just be a playful puppy in his former life, but he's making up for lost time now. His favorite thing is running zoomies in the yard with his foster sister Isabelle.

He was unsure of himself at first but has really blossomed with the help of his foster siblings. He's a sweet boy who's also got that playful side, great with all other dogs and cats, and is crate trained. He's a poodle mix who looks almost identical to another dog from the same rescue situation, and they think the two might actually be brothers.

Rudolph needs an experienced poodle or doodle home with at least one other adult dog and a fenced yard. The rescue wants to mention that while he's a poodle mix, they don't know how much poodle is actually in him, so they can't guarantee he'd be allergy friendly. Email the rescue directly or fill out an adoption application on their website.

**Rescue Me Ga Inc**
Dunwoody, GA 30338
adrienne@rescuemega.org | www.rmgdogs.org
scoring_notes:
• Young poodle mix appeals to families seeking trainable companion
• Hoarding rescue angle provides meaningful backstory
• Multiple adoption requirements may challenge placement efforts

blurb:
Midna and her brother Frankenwhisker are a bonded pair who came from a county shelter together. Midna is very scared in new environments right now, which makes sense considering she's been through a lot of changes recently. She's going to need someone patient who understands that she needs space and time to build her confidence.

The good news is she has her brother with her. They love to wrestle and play together, and having him around helps her feel secure. Two cats are almost always easier than one anyway, especially when they're already friends. They'll keep each other entertained and she'll have a familiar face while she adjusts to your home.

She will settle in and become a good companion once she feels safe. It's just going to take some time and a calm approach. If you've got the patience and space for two cats, you're getting a ready-made pair who already love each other.

Good Mews is a no-kill, cage-free shelter on Robinson Road in Marietta. You'll need to submit an inquiry for both cats and visit in person to meet them. Call or email to get the process started.
scoring_notes:
• Bonded pair angle appeals to readers wanting ready-made cat family
• Brother relationship adds heartwarming element to story
• Gaming reference name may connect with specific audience segment

blurb:
Brie is a 5-year-old orange and white cat who takes her meals very seriously. She's about 14.5 pounds right now and would benefit from scheduled feedings instead of free-choice grazing. She knows what she likes and when she wants it. Give her a gentle brush on her head and neck and she'll melt into the happiest purr you've ever heard. That's her love language right there.

She's a little shy when you first meet her, but once she settles in, she'll park herself next to your laptop while you work and politely request pets. She's fine with dogs as long as they give her space, but other cats make her nervous. She'd rather be the only cat in the house or live with one that minds its own business.

Brie does best in a calm environment with adults or older teens. High energy overwhelms her, so she's not the right fit for a chaotic household. If you're patient and ready to cheer on a sweetheart as she finds her confidence, Brie could be perfect for you.

Fill out an adoption application at Three Paws Rescue's website to get started. They're based in Chamblee and handle adoptions through their online process.
scoring_notes:
• Perfect for work-from-home readers seeking a calm office companion
• Clear care requirements make adoption decision straightforward
• Overweight senior cat may face longer wait times

blurb:
His name is Pork Tenderloin and he's a little misunderstood. He came to Good Mews from a county shelter where his personality probably didn't translate well in a stressful environment. He's sweet, but he likes attention on his own terms. When he's in the mood for affection, he'll come find you.

He gets overwhelmed more quickly than some cats, so he needs a calm environment and someone who won't push. If you're the type of person who lets cats come to you instead of forcing interaction, you two will probably get along great. He just needs someone who understands feline body language and respects his boundaries.

With the right person who gets him, he has the potential to form a really special bond. Sometimes the cats who are a little particular about their people end up being the most loyal once they choose you.

Good Mews is a no-kill, cage-free shelter on Robinson Road in Marietta. You'll need to submit an inquiry and visit in person to meet Pork Tenderloin. Call or email to get the process started.
scoring_notes:
• Memorable name creates instant reader connection and shareability
• Misunderstood personality appeals to cat-savvy adopters
• Potential for special bond resonates with experienced cat owners

blurb:
When Morrie came to the rescue in late fall, he was a mess. Extremely thin with a severe skin infection from what was likely years of neglect. It took months of care in his foster home to get this Italian Greyhound ready for adoption, but look at him now. He's about 18 pounds and very spunky for a dog who's estimated to be nine or ten years old.

He spends his days making his foster mom laugh because this boy can be so goofy and hilarious. He's great with all dogs and has bounced back from his rough start with the kind of resilience that makes you believe in second chances. Despite everything he's been through, he's still got that playful Italian Greyhound personality.

Morrie would love to find his very own person to show him what life is like as a beloved family member. Dogs like this who've been through neglect often become the most grateful, loyal companions once they realize they're safe. You can email the rescue directly or fill out an adoption application on their website.

**Rescue Me Ga Inc**
Dunwoody, GA 30338
adrienne@rescuemega.org | www.rmgdogs.org
scoring_notes:
• Dramatic transformation from neglected to thriving creates powerful visual story
• Senior Italian Greyhound appeals to breed enthusiasts and special needs adopters
• Months of recovery time indicates extended shelter stay

blurb:
Panda is seven years old but nobody told her to slow down. She came to the rescue with eight puppies after being held as part of a court case. Based on her scars, her past wasn't great, but that's all behind her now. She's beat heartworm disease, her puppies have all found homes, and she's ready to figure out what the good life looks like.

This girl still loves to romp and play with dogs half her age in her foster home. She's got energy and spirit, just needs dogs big enough to keep up with her. She's not a fan of small breeds but gets along great with bigger dogs and all people. Her favorite thing after a good play session is finding a comfy sofa to curl up on for a nap.

Panda would do best in a home with other large dogs and someone who appreciates a dog with some life experience. She's been through a lot and came out loving and playful on the other side. You can email the rescue directly or fill out an adoption application on their website.

**Rescue Me Ga Inc**
Dunwoody, GA 30338
adrienne@rescuemega.org | www.rmgdogs.org
scoring_notes:
• Inspirational survivor story showcases resilience after court case rescue
• Senior dog with high energy defies age stereotypes
• Specific large-dog household requirements may limit adopter pool

blurb:
Mandy showed up at an apartment complex with kittens, doing what she had to do to survive. Her babies have all been adopted and now it's her turn. She's about a year and a half old, incredibly gentle, and will roll over to let you pet her belly. That's not something most cats do, especially strays who've had it rough.

She's FIV positive, which sounds scarier than it actually is. She probably got it from a bite during mating, but FIV cats can live completely normal lives. It's only transmitted through deep bite wounds, not casual contact or sharing food bowls. Many people have FIV positive and negative cats living together without any problems.

Mandy is proof that street cats can be the most grateful, loving companions. She's been through a lot but still trusts humans completely. If you're looking for a sweet, gentle cat who knows what it means to be rescued, Mandy could be exactly what you're looking for.

Contact Patty's Kitties in Atlanta to meet her and learn more about the adoption process.
scoring_notes:
• Powerful rescue story of devoted mother cat now seeking her own family
• FIV status creates educational opportunity while extending wait time
• Exceptional trust and belly-showing behavior appeals to cat lovers

blurb:
Beignet is back at Good Mews, and it's not her fault. She was adopted from them as a kitten, but somehow ended up scared and alone at a county shelter. Good Mews brought her back when they found out, because that's what good shelters do for their animals.

She went straight into foster care to rebuild her confidence, and it worked. She's social now, loves being petted, and will sit close or lean against you when she wants attention. She's a social eater too, happiest munching when she has company. She can give gentle love bites when she gets overstimulated, so you'll want to watch her signals and give her space when she needs it.

She's ready for her second chance now. Sometimes the best cats are the ones who've had a rough patch and know what they're looking for in a home. If you're patient and understand cats, Beignet might be exactly what you didn't know you needed.

Good Mews is a no-kill, cage-free shelter on Robinson Road in Marietta. You'll need to submit an inquiry and visit in person to meet her. Call or email to get the process started.
scoring_notes:
• Heartwarming comeback story from scared to social builds reader empathy
• Now confident and affectionate after foster rehabilitation shows readiness
• Second-chance narrative creates urgency for deserving home
